mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive corporate earnings expectations and S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which supports near-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases during expansion regimes, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when both labor indicators move favorably.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion and corporate earnings growth, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and accelerating inflation defines a monetary tightening regime where higher discount rates and margin compression dominate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances during tightening regimes, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in walk-forward tests.
